title: Income inequality in market and disposable income across the world
subtitle: Unit 5 'Property and power: Mutual gains and conflict’ Section 5.12 'Measuring economic inequality' in The CORE Team, The Economy. Available at: https://tinyco.re/05121660 [Figure 5.16]
note: Disposable income Gini is displayed on the 'MAP' tab. CC-BY-ND-NC
caption:
Income inequality in market and disposable income across the world

Unit 5 'Property and power: Mutual gains and conflict’ Section 5.12 'Measuring economic inequality' in The CORE Team, The Economy. Available at: https://tinyco.re/05121660 [Figure 5.16]

This scatter plot compares market income Gini coefficients on the horizontal axis against disposable income Gini coefficients on the vertical axis for a selection of countries. Both axes range from 0 to roughly 0.7, with a diagonal reference line indicating where the two values would be equal. Most labeled data points sit above this diagonal line, showing that disposable income inequality is generally higher than market income inequality in this dataset. South Africa occupies the top-right position with the highest values on both axes, exceeding 0.6 for market Gini and approaching 0.65 for disposable Gini. China (People's Republic of), Brazil, and India cluster in the upper-right quadrant with market Gini values between 0.5 and 0.6 and disposable Gini values between 0.45 and 0.55. Chile and Mexico sit slightly lower, with market Gini around 0.45 to 0.5 and disposable Gini near 0.4 to 0.5. A dense cluster of European countries, including Iceland, Norway, Austria, Lithuania, and Bulgaria, occupies the lower-left to center region, with market Gini values mostly between 0.35 and 0.55 and disposable Gini values between 0.25 and 0.4. Australia and Korea appear near the center with market Gini around 0.45 and disposable Gini near 0.3 to 0.35. The legend assigns colors by region: North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania, though not all regions have labeled representatives visible in this view. The note specifies that disposable income Gini is displayed on the 'MAP' tab in the original source.
